metadata.title: MacPhail Family — Chapter 10
metadata.location: Argyle Shore, Prince Edward Island, Canada

people.name: John Granger MacPhail
people.notes: Third son of Angus McPhail (MP11415) and Christy McPhail (MP11437). Named for John Grainger of Charlottetown. Severely stricken with scarlet fever as a child causing hair loss. Kennison MacKinnon remembered him as a shower. Inherited family farm 1908. Built New House at Argyle Shore 1912, still in use. Probate of will granted 24 August 1950.

people.name: Angus A. MacPhail
people.notes: Named for grandfather Angus MacPhail. WWI 105th Battalion PEI Reg No 712664, enlisted 25 March 1916, served overseas. Moved to California about 1928, married and owned/operated a tavern. Died heart attack age 65. Probate granted PEI 9 March 1955. No children known.

people.name: Sarah A. MacPhail
people.notes: Named for grandmother Sarah MacEachern. Mentioned 1926 as being of Haverhill MA. Returned home 1927 to nurse mother and care for father after mother's death June 1928. Married George Clancy 1957, no children. George died November 1977.

people.name: Neil Granger MacPhail
people.notes: Named for grandfather Neil McEachern. Large family — details in Chapter 11.

people.name: Vina Isabelle MacPhail

spouses.name: Sterling MacLeod
spouses.notes: Son of John J. MacLeod and Flora MacKay of Riverdale Lot 30.

people.notes: By grandfather's will to receive $100.

spouses.name: Leslie Elliott Mitchell
spouses.notes: Son of Joseph William Mitchell and Hannah MacDonald of Nine Mile Creek PEI. Les was organiser and announcer of horse races on ice of West River before WWII.

people.notes: By grandfather's will to receive $100. Family moved to Milford CT early 1940s. Les worked at Chance-Vought Sikorsky Aircraft Stratford CT for 26 years. Retired 1967, returned to New Dominion PEI.

people.name: Frederick Melbourne MacPhail

spouses.name: Sarah Edith MacCannell
spouses.notes: Rev D.K. Ross was minister. Donald MacPhail (MP1143361) of Argyle Shore was bondsman. Edith was daughter of Neil MacCannell and Katie M. MacGregor of Nine Mile Creek PEI, born 22 May 1912 at New Dominion, baptised at New Dominion Presbyterian Church. Edith died 8 February 1998, buried Argyle Shore Cemetery.

people.notes: Fifth son of Granger MacPhail. Mentioned in Bonshaw A Stroll Through Its Past by Wm M and Elizabeth A Glen 1993. Went west on harvest train September 1926 with John M MacPhail (MP219713) and Robert MacPhail (MP219714) of Bonshaw. Took over family farm on Argyle Shore. Custodian of Argyle Shore Cemetery. FRED and Edith had six children.

people.notes: They had two children and have since divorced. Laurie and her daughter live at Argyle Shore PEI.

people.name: Duncan MacPhail

spouses.name: Doris Carolyn Tanton
spouses.notes: Daughter of Lewis Philkins and Ada Ellen Tanton of Charlottetown, born there 1905. Duncan was in Charlottetown Police Department many years, rising to rank of Sergeant. In 1973 Charlottetown Directory he and Doris lived at 229 University Ave Charlottetown; he was a night watchman for the city. Doris died 19 October 1990.

people.notes: Sixth son of Granger MacPhail.

people.name: Paul Philkins MacPhail
people.notes: Adopted son of Duncan MacPhail (MP114154a) and Doris Tanton. Paul married Shirley Savident about 1959. Had three children before this marriage ended. Married secondly Hannah Van't Veld about 1968. Hannah has two children from previous marriage to Mr Hendsbee. Paul and Hannah have two children of their own.

people.name: Cecil Kitchener MacPhail

spouses.name: Catherine Beverly Beaton
spouses.notes: Daughter of James Beaton and Melinda L MacEachern of Mt Herbert PEI, baptised 4 May 1941 at Bonshaw United Church.

people.notes: Seventh son of Granger MacPhail. Middle name honours Lord Kitchener, drowned in sinking of British warship 1916. Baptised 11 July 1954 at DeSable Free Church of Scotland, same day as his first three children. Cecil and Beverley live at Argyle Shore.
